\caption{\textbf{Frozen-erasure target-deployment test (BCI-IV-2a, LOSO; source-only fit, mean over 3 seeds $\times$ 9 folds; paired fold-cluster 95\% CI vs.\ full $Z$; target chance bAcc $0.25$).} Each eraser is fit on source, applied to source and the held-out target, a linear task head is trained on transformed source, and evaluated on the transformed target; the target is never used for fitting, selection, or calibration. \emph{No eraser improves target balanced accuracy} on either backbone (all $\Delta$bAcc CIs overlap or fall below zero; INLP collapses the task to chance). The only positive movement is a small NLL reduction, but it is not erasure-specific: same-$k$ \emph{random} removal produces a same-sign reduction without erasing subject identity, and INLP's NLL drop coincides with task collapse --- not a domain-removal benefit.}
